core_loop.description: Both participants fill an Action Queue upfront. Actions resolve one at a time, fastest first.
steps: Step_1_Queue_Setup: Each participant fills up to 3 Action Slots.
Step_2_Speed_Check: Calculate Current Speed for both Top Actions.
Step_3_Interaction_Check: Compare Top Actions for any interactions (see interaction_system).
Step_4_Execution: Highest Speed Action generates and delivers its Payload.
Step_5_Cleanup: Remove resolved Action(s) from Queue. Consume flagged Tags.
Step_6_Repeat: Return to Step 2 until all Queues are empty.

battle_loop.description: The concrete function-level flow that runs after all actions are declared. Loops until all queues are empty, then runs turn cleanup.

functions.name: SpeedCheckAllAvailableActions()
functions.description: Re-sorts all top actions every loop. Priority flags override speed ordering.
sort_rules: 1. Actions flagged NEXT are pulled to the top regardless of speed.
2. Actions flagged SKIP are pulled out and nullified.
3. Remaining actions sorted by Current_Speed descending.
functions.returns: Ordered action list for this loop iteration.

functions.name: InteractionCheck(FastestAction, 2ndFastestAction)
functions.description: Compares the top two actions for any qualifying interactions. Stamps priority flags and modifiers onto actions. Does not execute anything.

returns.notes: actionB gets a priority_flag=NEXT if it needs to execute before the next speed sort.

functions.name: ExecuteAction(FastestAction)
functions.description: Runs the full payload pipeline for the top action only. One action per loop. See payload_system for pipeline details.
functions.notes: This is the most complex function. Tag injection, damage calc, status effects all live here.

functions.name: ActionCleanup(ExecutedAction)
functions.description: Scoped to the acting character. Removes the executed action from the queue. Clears priority_flag. Ticks or removes tags with Action duration.

functions.name: TurnResultCleanup()
functions.description: Broad sweep across all characters. Handles tags with End_of_Turn duration (e.g. COMBO_STACK reset).

speed_system.notes: Slot penalties punish front-loading a queue with slow actions. Fast actions in later slots can still compete.

priority_flag.description: A temporary flag stamped onto an action by InteractionCheck. Overrides speed sorting for one loop. Cleared by ActionCleanup.

values.null: No override — sort by speed normally.
values.NEXT: Force this action to the top of the next sort, ignoring speed.
values.SKIP: Remove this action from the queue without executing it.

interaction_system.description: Before payload delivery, the engine compares both Top Actions for any qualifying interactions. Multiple interactions can resolve on the same pair. Interactions are evaluated in priority order.
interaction_system.evaluation_order: Sequential — checked top to bottom. Multiple can fire on the same pair.

known_interactions.base_result: Both Actions Nullified — no damage dealt.

modifiers.effect: Even on Clash nullification, arc damage still delivers to target.
modifiers.note: Tag conditions can append effects on top of the base Clash result.

known_interactions.notes: Clash does not always mean nothing happens. Tags riding on either action can still fire.

trigger_conditions: Opposing action is slower by more than threshold (TBD)
known_interactions.base_result: Slower action is removed from queue before it resolves. No counter-payload.
known_interactions.notes: Threshold for Interrupt suppression TBD. May need its own speed gap rule.

trigger_conditions: Defending participant has an active Stance Tag (e.g. RIPOSTE_STANCE)
Incoming action triggers the Stance's trigger condition (e.g. On_Hit)
known_interactions.base_result: Stance fires its reaction effect (negate damage, deal counter damage, etc.).
known_interactions.notes: Counter is a Reaction — it hooks into the Interaction Check when a Stance Tag is active.

open_questions: What is the speed gap threshold for INTERRUPT to suppress an action?
Can two interactions fire simultaneously (e.g. CLASH + tag effect on same pair)?
Are there interactions that don't involve the opposing action at all (self-interactions)?

payload_system.description: Resolved actions generate a Payload Object. Tags modify this object in phases before delivery.

phases.description: Generate base Payload from card stats. Populate damages array from card target tags.
Imbue tags fire here. Modify what the attack IS — convert elements, add properties, add status effects. By end of this phase the payload identity is locked.
phases.consume_rule: Consume on trigger regardless of condition.

phases.description: Injector tags fire here. Scale how hard the attack hits. Operates on the fully-formed payload from INJECT phase.
phases.consume_rule: Only consume if condition passes. If condition fails, tag stays in pool.
examples: MAGIC_CHARGE boosts all damages if payload.type == MAGIC
FIRE_CHARGE boosts only damages where element == FIRE

phases.description: Final payload hits the target. Resistance, weakness, and shield tags fire here reacting to the fully calculated payload.
Fires after delivery. Receives hit_result from DELIVERY phase. Handlers use hit_result to decide whether to trigger.
examples: COMBO_STACK increments only if hit_result == HIT

payload_structure.type: MAGIC | PHYSICAL | (extensible) — used by Injector conditions
payload_structure.damages: List of { element, power } — supports multiple damage types simultaneously

tag_system.description: Tags are persistent or reactive modifiers attached to a participant. They are generated by cards and consumed after use (unless flagged otherwise).

architecture.pattern: Data-driven registry. Tags are pure data — labels with parameters. All logic lives in the battle system handler registry.
architecture.tag_role: A tag is a message. It tells the battle system what handler to invoke, and carries the parameters that handler needs.
architecture.registry_role: The battle system maintains a handler map. When a tag is evaluated, it looks up tag_name in the registry and executes the corresponding handler.

example.benefit: Adding a new tag never touches existing code. Each handler is isolated and lives separately.

tag_categories.behavior: Fires at INJECT phase. Modifies what the attack IS — converts elements, adds properties or status effects. Consumed on trigger.
Fires at DAMAGE_CALC phase. Scales damage conditionally based on payload.type or damage element. Only consumed if condition passes.
tag_categories.condition_rule: Each Injector declares its own condition. If condition fails, tag stays in pool and waits for a qualifying action.
examples: MAGIC_CHARGE — boosts all damages if payload.type == MAGIC
FIRE_CHARGE — boosts only damages where element == FIRE

tag_categories.behavior: Waits for a specific incoming trigger (On_Hit, On_Targeted). Fires a reaction. Consumed after use.
Persistent stacking buff. Fires at a recurring trigger (Start_of_Turn). Not consumed. Has max stack cap.

open_questions: COMBO_STACK exists in the glossary but no card generates it yet — is this intentional?
Can multiple Imbue tags stack on one attack, or does only one fire per payload?
Do Stance tags also hook into the Interaction Check, or only into payload delivery?

card_system.description: Cards are the actions participants queue. A card is pure data — it declares what tags to apply and to whom. It does not contain logic.

card_structure.name: String
card_structure.tag_type: List — innate properties of the action (e.g. SPELL, MAGIC, PHYSICAL). Used by Interaction Check for clash/counter eligibility.
card_structure.cost: Object — resource costs as key-value pairs. e.g. {WIZARD_MANA: 100, HEALTH: 25}. One entry per resource.

tags.self: Effects applied to the acting character only.
tags.target: Effects applied to a single selected enemy.
tags.all_enemies: Effects applied to every opposing character.
tags.all_allies: Effects applied to all teammates including self.
tags.all: Effects applied to every character in battle including self.
tags.random_enemy: Effects applied to a randomly selected enemy.
tags.random_ally: Effects applied to a randomly selected teammate.

card_structure.notes: A single card can populate multiple targeting keys simultaneously.

tag_object_structure.tag_name: String — maps to a handler in the battle system registry.
tag_object_structure.parameters: Any additional fields the handler needs (e.g. power, multiplier, type). Varies per tag.

card_system.notes: tag_type on the card is used by the Interaction Check for eligibility checks. Tags on the card generate effects — self tags buff the caster, target tags deliver to the opponent.

ReadCard.description: A UI-only function. Flattens all tags across all targeting groups into a readable summary for display in hand. Does not touch battle logic.

returns.name: String
returns.effects: List of { targeting, description } — one entry per tag across all targeting keys

ReadCard.how: Iterates every targeting key (self, target, all_enemies, etc.), collects all tags inside, looks up each tag_name in the ui_registry to get its description string.

registry_architecture.battle_registry: Maps tag_name → handler function. Used during battle execution. e.g. battle_registry["MAGIC_CHARGE"] → MagicChargeHandler(payload, character, tag)
registry_architecture.ui_registry: Maps tag_name → describe function. Used by ReadCard for UI display only. e.g. ui_registry["MAGIC_CHARGE"] → MagicChargeDescribe(tag) → "Boost next MAGIC attack by 20%"
registry_architecture.separation: These two registries are completely independent. Battle system never touches ui_registry. UI never touches battle_registry.

implementation_notes.description: Decisions locked in during design session. These resolve the open questions that existed in v1.2 and represent the canonical implementation.

speed_system.slot_index: 0-based. Slot 1 = index 0 (no penalty), Slot 2 = index 1, Slot 3 = index 2.
speed_system.total_action_slots: A character stat, not a global constant. Default is 3. FORWARD_INITIATIVE increases this value per stack.
speed_system.forward_initiative_effect: Each stack of FORWARD_INITIATIVE adds 1 to total_action_slots. This softens the per-slot penalty rather than adding a free slot. Hard cap: TBD.
speed_system.tie_breaking: If two actions have equal calc_speed after sorting, order is undefined (implementation may use stable sort or insertion order).

clash_system.trigger: Both actions must carry 'CLASH' in their tag_type array AND belong to opposing participants AND have speed difference <= 2.
clash_system.note: CLASH is opt-in via tag_type — not all PHYSICAL actions clash. Cards must explicitly declare tag_type: ['PHYSICAL', 'CLASH'] to be eligible.
clash_system.base_result: Both actions NULLIFY — no damage delivered from either action's base payload.
clash_system.tag_riders: Imbue tags already injected onto the payload before InteractionCheck are evaluated separately. Example: ELECTRIC_IMBUE arc damage still fires even on a Clash nullification. This is not yet fully specified — treat as open.
clash_system.multiple_interactions: Yes — multiple interactions can fire on the same pair sequentially. E.g. a CLASH can co-fire with a tag-driven effect.

interrupt_system.trigger: Action carries Property=INTERRUPT (added via an Imbue tag like DIVING_STRIKE) AND the acting character's action is faster than the opposing action.
interrupt_system.speed_gap_threshold: Any positive speed difference is sufficient — no minimum gap required. If actionA.calc_speed > actionB.calc_speed and actionA has INTERRUPT, actionB is flagged SKIP.
interrupt_system.result: Opposing action flagged SKIP — removed from queue without executing. actionA proceeds normally.
interrupt_system.note: INTERRUPT is a property on the payload, not a tag_type on the card. It is added during the IMBUE phase by an Imbue tag.

counter_system.stance_hook: Stance tags hook into both InteractionCheck AND payload DELIVERY — depending on stance type.
counter_system.interaction_check_stances: Stances with trigger On_Targeted fire during InteractionCheck before the payload pipeline runs. They can NULLIFY incoming action or flag it.
counter_system.delivery_stances: Stances with trigger On_Hit fire during DELIVERY phase after damage is calculated. They react to the hit result.
counter_system.example: RIPOSTE_STANCE: On_Hit trigger — fires during DELIVERY, negates damage and deals counter damage to attacker.

imbue_tag_stacking.rule: Only one Imbue tag fires per payload. The first Imbue tag in the pool fires and is consumed. Subsequent Imbue tags in the pool wait for the next action.
imbue_tag_stacking.rationale: Multiple element conversions on one attack would be contradictory. First in wins.
imbue_tag_stacking.note: This may be revisited — stacking Imbue effects (e.g. FIRE + ICE = STEAM) is a possible future mechanic but is not implemented.

injector_tag_stacking.rule: Multiple Injector tags CAN stack and all fire during DAMAGE_CALC phase. Each checks its own condition independently.
injector_tag_stacking.onApply: Tags with an onApply handler in the registry merge into existing stack rather than adding a duplicate entry. e.g. MAGIC_CHARGE stacks via stack_count.
injector_tag_stacking.consume_rule: Each Injector only consumes if its own condition passes. A FIRE_CHARGE tag does not consume if the payload has no FIRE damage.

tag_duration_system.action_duration: Tags cleared by ActionCleanup after the action that generated them resolves. Used for within-turn effects.
tag_duration_system.end_of_turn: Tags with reset='END_OF_TURN' are removed by TurnResultCleanup after all queues are empty. e.g. MOMENTUM.
tag_duration_system.tick_turn: Tags with reset='TICK_TURN' decrement duration each TurnResultCleanup. Removed when duration reaches 0. e.g. SPEED_BOOST.
tag_duration_system.persistent: Tags with no reset field (e.g. COMBO_STACK) are never automatically removed. Must be cleared by specific game events.

combo_stack.status: Intentionally unimplemented in cards for now. The tag and handler exist in the registry. No card currently generates it — it is reserved for future character/card design.
combo_stack.behavior: POST_ATTACK phase. Increments stack_count only on hit_result == HIT. Never consumed. Resets via TurnResultCleanup (END_OF_TURN).

registry_architecture.onApply: Optional. Called when tag is added to pool via addTagToPool(). Handles stacking logic. If absent, tag is pushed directly to pool.

handler_signatures.INJECT: handler(payload, character, tag) → { payload, consumed: bool }
handler_signatures.DELIVERY: handler(payload, character, tag) → { payload, consumed: bool }
handler_signatures.POST_ATTACK: handler(payload, character, tag, hit_result) → { consumed: bool }

registry_architecture.ui_registry: Separate registry. Maps tag_name → describe(tag) → String. Used by ReadCard() for UI display only. Not yet implemented.

payload_type.derivation: payload.type is set from the card's tag_type array at queue time. If tag_type includes 'MAGIC', payload_type = 'MAGIC'. Otherwise payload_type = 'PHYSICAL'.
payload_type.used_by: Injector conditions. e.g. MAGIC_CHARGE checks payload.type == 'MAGIC'.

targeting.current_scope: 1v1 only. Player always targets the single enemy. Enemy always targets the player.
targeting.multi_target: all_enemies, all_allies, random_enemy, random_ally targeting keys exist in card structure but are not implemented in the engine. Reserved for future multi-participant expansion.
targeting.team_vs_team: Not designed yet. Flagged as open design question.

hit_result.current_derivation: HIT if total_damage > 0 after DELIVERY. MISS if total_damage == 0. BLOCKED not yet implemented (requires SHIELD or evasion mechanics).
hit_result.used_by: POST_ATTACK handlers. e.g. COMBO_STACK only increments on HIT.

open_design_questions: What is the hard cap on total_action_slots from FORWARD_INITIATIVE stacking?
How does ELECTRIC_IMBUE arc damage calculate during a Clash — flat value or % of base power?
Can Imbue tags stack to create combined element effects (e.g. FIRE + ICE = STEAM)? Currently first-in-wins.
What other Interaction types exist beyond Clash, Interrupt, and Counter?
How does targeting work in a multi-participant team vs team scenario?
What triggers BLOCKED hit_result — is this a SHIELD tag, an evasion stat, or both?
How does FORWARD_INITIATIVE interact with the slot penalty formula when total_action_slots increases mid-turn?
Are there self-interactions (tags that fire based on the owner's own action type, not the opponent's)?
